relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks repair where trust has been broken.
Deceiving a companion is unfaithfulness against YHWH.
Lying over what was lost or entrusted stains the soul.
The wrongfully held thing must be returned.
Restitution adds a fifth and goes to the one harmed.
Only then is reparation brought near to YHWH.
Covering is made, and forgiveness is given.
YHWH speaks the altar’s tending.
The burnt offering remains on the hearth through night.
The priest lifts ashes in linen garments.
Ashes are carried outside to a clean place.
Morning by morning the altar fire is tended.
The continual fire is not allowed to go out.
The grain offering has its instruction.
A memorial handful rises with oil and frankincense.
The remainder feeds the priests in holy place.
The portion is most holy.
Priestly sons may eat the holy portion.
YHWH speaks the priestly grain offering.
Morning and evening flour mark anointing.
Oil and griddle transform flour into fragrance.
The anointed priest continues the whole offering.
The priestly grain offering is wholly given.
YHWH speaks the purification offering instruction.
The offering is most holy before YHWH.
The priest who offers it eats in holy place.
Contact with its flesh makes holy; blood/life is washed carefully.
Vessels touched by the holy are handled differently.
Priestly males may eat what is most holy.
When blood/life goes inward for covering, the offering is burned, not eaten.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 6 says repair is concrete: return what was taken, add restoration, tend the fire, carry the ashes, and keep holy service from becoming theater.
